'''八上比売'''（やかみひめ、'''八上姫'''）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[因幡国]][[八上郡 (鳥取県)|八上郡]]の姫。[[大国主神]]の最初の[[妃]]。大国主神との間に[[木俣神]]を儲ける。
== 祀られている神社 == *[[売沼神社]]（[[鳥取県]][[鳥取市]]） *[[都波只知上神社]]（鳥取県鳥取市） *[[島御子神社]]（[[長崎県]][[対馬市]]）
==関連項目== *[[因幡の白兎]]
{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やかみひめ}} [[Category:日本の神 (人物神 記紀)]] [[Category:女神]]

{{出典の明記|date=2016年1月5日 (火) 15:08 (UTC)}} '''八上比売'''（やかみひめ、'''八上姫'''）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[因幡国]][[八上郡 (鳥取県)|八上郡]]の姫。[[大国主神]]の最初の[[妃]]。大国主神との間に[[木俣神]]を儲ける。

==関連項目== *[[因幡の白兎]]

revision.text:
{{神道}} '''八上比売'''（やかみひめ、'''八上姫'''）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[因幡国]][[八上郡 (鳥取県)|八上郡]]の姫。[[大国主神]]の最初の[[妃]]。大国主神との間に[[木俣神]]をもうける。
==神話での記述== 大国主神と大勢の兄弟神（八十神）は、八上比売への求婚のために因幡国へと向かった。 その途中、大国主神は[[因幡の白兎]]と出会いこれを救う。 救われた白兎は「あなたの求婚は成功するでしょう」という予言を残し、この予言は的中する。 <ref> [[竹田恒泰]]『現代語古事記 ポケット版』[[学研プラス]]、2016年。ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6</ref>
== 祀られている神社 == * [[売沼神社]]（[[鳥取県]][[鳥取市]]） * [[都波只知上神社]]（鳥取県鳥取市） * [[島御子神社]]（[[長崎県]][[対馬市]]）
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
==関連項目== * [[因幡の白兎]] * [[日本の神の一覧]]
{{日本神話}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やかみひめ}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:女神]]

{{神道}} '''八上比売'''（やかみひめ、'''八上姫'''）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[因幡国]][[八上郡 (鳥取県)|八上郡]]の姫。[[大国主神]]の最初の[[妃]]。大国主神との間に[[木俣神]]をもうける。
==神話での記述== 大国主神と大勢の兄弟神（八十神）は、八上比売への求婚のために因幡国へと向かった。 その途中、大国主神は[[因幡の白兎]]と出会いこれを救う。 救われた白兎は「あなたの求婚は成功するでしょう」という予言を残し、この予言は的中する。 <ref> [[竹田恒泰]]『現代語古事記 ポケット版』[[学研プラス]]、2016年。ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6</ref>
== 祀られている神社 == * [[売沼神社]]（[[鳥取県]][[鳥取市]]） * [[都波只知上神社]]（鳥取県鳥取市） * [[島御子神社]]（[[長崎県]][[対馬市]]）
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
==関連項目== * [[因幡の白兎]] * [[日本の神の一覧]]
{{日本神話}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やかみひめ}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:女神]] [[Category:国津神]]

{{神道}} '''八上比売'''（やかみひめ、'''八上姫'''）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[因幡国]][[八上郡 (鳥取県)|八上郡]]の姫。[[大国主神]]の最初の[[妃]]。大国主神との間に[[木俣神]]をもうける。
==神話での記述== 大国主神と大勢の兄弟神（八十神）は、八上比売への求婚のために因幡国へと向かった。 その途中、大国主神は[[因幡の白兎]]と出会いこれを救う。 救われた白兎は「あなたの求婚は成功するでしょう」という予言を残し、この予言は的中する。 <ref>[[竹田恒泰]]『現代語古事記 ポケット版』[[学研プラス]]、2016年。ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6</ref>
== 祀られている神社 == * [[売沼神社]]（[[鳥取県]][[鳥取市]]） * [[都波只知上神社]]（鳥取県鳥取市） * [[島御子神社]]（[[長崎県]][[対馬市]]） *八上姫神社(島根県出雲市)
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
==関連項目== * [[因幡の白兎]] * [[日本の神の一覧]]
{{日本神話}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やかみひめ}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:女神]] [[Category:国津神]]

{{神道}} '''八上比売'''（やかみひめ、'''八上姫'''）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[因幡国]][[八上郡 (鳥取県)|八上郡]]の[[女神]]。[[大国主神]]の最初の[[妃]]。大国主神との間に[[木俣神]]をもうける。
==神話での記述== 大国主神と大勢の兄弟神（八十神）は、八上比売への求婚のために因幡国へと向かった。 その途中、大国主神は[[因幡の白兎]]と出会いこれを救う。 救われた白兎は「あなたの求婚は成功するでしょう」という予言を残し、この予言は的中する。 <ref>[[竹田恒泰]]『現代語古事記 ポケット版』[[学研プラス]]、2016年。ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6</ref>
== 祀られている神社 == * [[売沼神社]]（[[鳥取県]][[鳥取市]]） * [[都波只知上神社]]（鳥取県鳥取市） * [[島御子神社]]（[[長崎県]][[対馬市]]） *八上姫神社(島根県出雲市)
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
==関連項目== * [[因幡の白兎]] * [[日本の神の一覧]]
{{日本神話}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やかみひめ}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:女神]] [[Category:国津神]]

{{神道}} '''八上比売'''（やかみひめ、'''八上姫'''）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[因幡国]][[八上郡 (鳥取県)|八上郡]]の[[女神]]。[[大国主神]]の最初の[[妃]]。大国主神との間に[[木俣神]]をもうける。
==神話での記述== 大国主神と大勢の兄弟神（八十神）は、八上比売への求婚のために因幡国へと向かった。 その途中、大国主神は[[因幡の白兎]]と出会いこれを救う。 救われた白兎は「あなたの求婚は成功するでしょう」という予言を残し、この予言は的中する。 <ref>[[竹田恒泰]]『現代語古事記 ポケット版』[[学研プラス]]、2016年。ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6</ref>
== 祀る神社 == * [[売沼神社]]（[[鳥取県]][[鳥取市]][[河原町]]） * [[都波只知上神社]]（鳥取県鳥取市河原町） * [[島御子神社]]（[[長崎県]][[対馬市]][[豊玉町]]） * [[八上姫神社]]（[[島根県]][[出雲市]][[斐川町]]）
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
==関連項目== * [[因幡の白兎]] * [[日本の神の一覧]]
{{日本神話}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やかみひめ}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:女神]] [[Category:国津神]]

{{神道}} '''八上比売'''（やかみひめ、'''八上姫'''）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[因幡国]][[八上郡 (鳥取県)|八上郡]]の[[女神]]。[[大国主神]]の最初の[[妃]]。大国主神との間に[[木俣神]]をもうける。
== 概要 == 『[[古事記]]』において'''八上比売'''、『[[先代旧事本紀]]』では'''稲羽八上姫'''と表記されている。
[[大穴牟遅神]]の大勢の兄弟神（[[八十神]]）は大穴牟遅神を荷物持ちとして、八上比売への求婚のために[[因幡国]]へと向かった。その途中、負傷した挙げ句八十神たちに騙されて、傷が痛んだ兎に大穴牟遅神は出会いこれを救う（[[因幡の白兎]]）。救われた白兎は「あなたの求婚は成功するでしょう」という予言を残し、この予言は的中する<ref>[[竹田恒泰]]『現代語古事記 ポケット版』[[学研プラス]]、2016年。ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6</ref>。
八上比売は八十神たちの求婚をはね除け、大穴牟遅神との結婚を宣言するが、妬んだ八十神たちの謀略によって大穴牟遅神は二度殺されてしまう。母の[[刺国若比売]]などに救われた大穴牟遅神は[[根の国|根之堅洲国]]に逃れ、そこで受けた[[須佐之男命]]の試練を乗り越え、[[須勢理毘売命]]を娶り、大国主神となって八十神たちを平定する。その後大国主神は八上比売を宮殿に迎え入れるが、正妻となった須勢理毘売命の嫉妬を恐れ、生まれたばかりの子を木の俣に挟んで因幡の国へ帰ってしまう。
売沼神社に近い[[嶽古墳]]が八上比売の神陵とされる他、各地に八上比売の関連伝承が存在する。
「八上」は現在の「[[八頭郡]]」、「命」が着かないことは巫女性を表すと解し、名義は「因幡国八上郡の豪族の娘である巫女」と考えられる<ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref>。
== 系譜 == [[画像:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|大国主の系図（『古事記』による）。青は男神、赤は女神、黄は性別不詳]] [[大国主神]]との間に[[木俣神]]を生んでいる。
== 祀る神社 == * [[売沼神社]]（[[鳥取県]][[鳥取市]][[河原町]]） * [[都波只知上神社]]（鳥取県鳥取市河原町） * [[島御子神社]]（[[長崎県]][[対馬市]][[豊玉町]]） * [[八上姫神社]]（[[島根県]][[出雲市]][[斐川町]]）
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
==関連項目== * [[因幡の白兎]] * [[日本の神の一覧]]
{{日本神話}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やかみひめ}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:女神]] [[Category:国津神]]

{{神道}} {{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 八上比売 | 先代= | 次代= | 神祇 = <!-- 天神、地祇、天津神、国津神など -->[[国津神]] | 全名 = 八上比売 | 別名 = 稲羽八上姫 | 別称 = | 神階 = | 神格 = <!-- 太陽、月、山、海など司るもの -->不明 | 陵所 = | 父 = 不明 | 母 = | 親 = <!-- まぐあいによって生まれない場合 --> | 配偶者 = [[大国主神]] | 子 = [[木俣神]] | 宮 = | 神社 = [[売沼神社]]等 }}

== 概要 == 『[[古事記]]』において'''八上比売'''、『[[先代旧事本紀]]』では'''稲羽八上姫'''と表記されている。
[[大穴牟遅神]]の大勢の兄弟神（[[八十神]]）は大穴牟遅神を荷物持ちとして、八上比売への求婚のために[[因幡国]]へと向かった。その途中、負傷した挙げ句八十神たちに騙されて、傷が痛んだ兎に大穴牟遅神は出会いこれを救う（[[因幡の白兎]]）。救われた白兎は「あなたの求婚は成功するでしょう」という予言を残し、この予言は的中する<ref>[[竹田恒泰]]『現代語古事記 ポケット版』[[学研プラス]]、2016年。ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6</ref>。
八上比売は八十神たちの求婚をはね除け、大穴牟遅神との結婚を宣言するが、妬んだ八十神たちの謀略によって大穴牟遅神は二度殺されてしまう。母の[[刺国若比売]]などに救われた大穴牟遅神は[[根の国|根之堅洲国]]に逃れ、そこで受けた[[須佐之男命]]の試練を乗り越え、[[須勢理毘売命]]を娶り、大国主神となって八十神たちを平定する。その後大国主神は八上比売を宮殿に迎え入れるが、正妻となった須勢理毘売命の嫉妬を恐れ、生まれたばかりの子を木の俣に挟んで因幡の国へ帰ってしまう。
売沼神社に近い[[嶽古墳]]が八上比売の神陵とされる他、各地に八上比売の関連伝承が存在する。
「八上」は現在の「[[八頭郡]]」、「命」が着かないことは巫女性を表すと解し、名義は「因幡国八上郡の豪族の娘である巫女」と考えられる<ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref>。
== 系譜 == [[画像:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|大国主の系図（『古事記』による）。青は男神、赤は女神、黄は性別不詳]] [[大国主神]]との間に[[木俣神]]を生んでいる。

売沼神社に近い[[嶽古墳]]が八上比売の神陵とされる他、各地に八上比売の関連伝承が存在する。

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Infobox Japanese deity | name = Yagami Hime | predecessor = | successor = | type = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | full name = 八上比売 (Yagami Hime) | other names = 稲羽八上姫 (Inaba Yagami Hime) | alternate = | rank = | role = Unknown | mausoleum = | father = Unknown | mother = | relatives = | consort = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}} | children = {{ill|Kimata..."
revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese deity | name = Yagami Hime | predecessor = | successor = | type = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | full name = 八上比売 (Yagami Hime) | other names = 稲羽八上姫 (Inaba Yagami Hime) | alternate = | rank = | role = Unknown | mausoleum = | father = Unknown | mother = | relatives = | consort = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}} | children = {{ill|Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神}} | shrine = {{ill|Ushinuma Shrine|ja|売沼神社}} and others }}
'''Yagami Hime''' (八上比売 / 八上姫, also read as Yagami Hime or Yakami Hime) is a goddess from {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}, associated with {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|因幡国}} in {{ill|Yazu District|ja|八上郡}}. She was the first consort of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}.
== Overview ==
In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, her name is written as '''Yagami Hime''', while in the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, she is referred to as '''Inaba Yagami Hime'''.
Ōnamuchi no Mikoto (Ōkuninushi) traveled to {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|因幡国}} as a servant to his many brothers, the {{ill|Yasogami|ja|八十神}}, who were seeking Yagami Hime’s hand in marriage. Along the way, Ōnamuchi encountered a wounded hare that had been tricked and harmed by the Yasogami. He healed the hare’s injuries, and in return, the hare prophesied that Ōnamuchi would succeed in his courtship—a prediction that proved accurate.<ref>Takeuchi, Tsuneyasu. ''Modern Language Kojiki: Pocket Edition.'' Gakken Plus, 2016. ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6</ref>
Yagami Hime rejected the Yasogami's proposals and chose to marry Ōnamuchi instead. Jealous, the Yasogami schemed to kill him twice. However, Ōnamuchi was revived by his mother, {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売}}, and fled to the {{ill|Netherworld|ja|根の国}}. After overcoming trials imposed by {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}}, Ōnamuchi married {{ill|Suseribime|ja|須勢理毘売命}} and returned to conquer the Yasogami, becoming the great deity Ōkuninushi. Although Yagami Hime was welcomed to his palace, she feared the jealousy of Suseribime, the principal wife, and fled back to Inaba, leaving her newborn son, {{ill|Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神}}, in a tree fork.
The nearby {{ill|Dake Kofun|ja|嶽古墳}} is considered Yagami Hime’s burial site. Numerous legends about her exist across Japan.
The name "Yagami" likely refers to present-day {{ill|Yazu District|ja|八頭郡}}, and her lack of the suffix "Mikoto" suggests she might have been a priestess of Inaba.<ref>Shinchosha, ''Shincho Nihon Koten Shusei Kojiki''</ref>
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (according to the ''Kojiki''). Blue indicates male deities, red indicates female deities, and yellow indicates unspecified gender.]] Yagami Hime bore one child with Ōkuninushi, {{ill|Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神}}.
== Shrines == * {{ill|Ushinuma Shrine|ja|売沼神社}} ({{ill|Tottori|ja|鳥取県鳥取市河原町}}) * {{ill|Tsunamitada Kamijinja|ja|都波只知上神社}} (Tottori Prefecture) * {{ill|Shimamiko Shrine|ja|島御子神社}} ({{ill|Tsushima|ja|長崎県対馬市}}) * {{ill|Yagami Hime Shrine|ja|八上姫神社}} ({{ill|Izumo|ja|島根県出雲市斐川町}})
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Hare of Inaba|ja|因幡の白兎}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧}} * {{ill|Dake Kofun|ja|嶽古墳}}
{{Japanese mythology}} {{Shinto 横}} {{Shinto-stub}}
[[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Goddesses]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]]
[[ja:八上比売]]

Blue indicates male deities, red indicates female deities, and yellow indicates unspecified gender.]] Yagami Hime bore one child with Ōkuninushi, {{ill|Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神}}.

{{infobox deity | name = Yagami Hime | predecessor = | successor = | type = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | full name = 八上比売 (Yagami Hime) | other names = 稲羽八上姫 (Inaba Yagami Hime) | alternate = | rank = | role = Unknown | mausoleum = | father = Unknown | mother = | relatives = | consort = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}} | children = {{ill|Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神}} | shrine = {{ill|Ushinuma Shrine|ja|売沼神社}} and others }}
'''Yagami Hime''' (八上比売 / 八上姫, also read as Yagami Hime or Yakami Hime) is a goddess from {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}, associated with {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|因幡国}} in {{ill|Yazu District|ja|八上郡}}. She was the first consort of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}.
== Overview ==
In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, her name is written as '''Yagami Hime''', while in the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, she is referred to as '''Inaba Yagami Hime'''.
Ōnamuchi no Mikoto (Ōkuninushi) traveled to {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|因幡国}} as a servant to his many brothers, the {{ill|Yasogami|ja|八十神}}, who were seeking Yagami Hime’s hand in marriage. Along the way, Ōnamuchi encountered a wounded hare that had been tricked and harmed by the Yasogami. He healed the hare’s injuries, and in return, the hare prophesied that Ōnamuchi would succeed in his courtship—a prediction that proved accurate.<ref>Takeuchi, Tsuneyasu. ''Modern Language Kojiki: Pocket Edition.'' Gakken Plus, 2016. ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6</ref>
Yagami Hime rejected the Yasogami's proposals and chose to marry Ōnamuchi instead. Jealous, the Yasogami schemed to kill him twice. However, Ōnamuchi was revived by his mother, {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売}}, and fled to the {{ill|Netherworld|ja|根の国}}. After overcoming trials imposed by {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}}, Ōnamuchi married {{ill|Suseribime|ja|須勢理毘売命}} and returned to conquer the Yasogami, becoming the great deity Ōkuninushi. Although Yagami Hime was welcomed to his palace, she feared the jealousy of Suseribime, the principal wife, and fled back to Inaba, leaving her newborn son, {{ill|Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神}}, in a tree fork.
The nearby {{ill|Dake Kofun|ja|嶽古墳}} is considered Yagami Hime’s burial site. Numerous legends about her exist across Japan.
The name "Yagami" likely refers to present-day {{ill|Yazu District|ja|八頭郡}}, and her lack of the suffix "Mikoto" suggests she might have been a priestess of Inaba.<ref>Shinchosha, ''Shincho Nihon Koten Shusei Kojiki''</ref>
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (according to the ''Kojiki''). Blue indicates male deities, red indicates female deities, and yellow indicates unspecified gender.]] Yagami Hime bore one child with Ōkuninushi, {{ill|Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神}}.

'''Yagami Hime''' (八上比売 / 八上姫, also read as Yagami Hime or Yakami Hime) is a goddess from {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}, associated with {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|因幡国}} in {{ill|Yazu District|ja|八上郡}}. She was the first consort of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}.
== Overview ==
In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, her name is written as '''Yagami Hime''', while in the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, she is referred to as '''Inaba Yagami Hime'''.
Ōnamuchi no Mikoto (Ōkuninushi) traveled to {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|因幡国}} as a servant to his many brothers, the {{ill|Yasogami|ja|八十神}}, who were seeking Yagami Hime’s hand in marriage. Along the way, Ōnamuchi encountered a wounded hare that had been tricked and harmed by the Yasogami. He healed the hare’s injuries, and in return, the hare prophesied that Ōnamuchi would succeed in his courtship—a prediction that proved accurate.<ref>Takeuchi, Tsuneyasu. ''Modern Language Kojiki: Pocket Edition.'' Gakken Plus, 2016. ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6</ref>
Yagami Hime rejected the Yasogami's proposals and chose to marry Ōnamuchi instead. Jealous, the Yasogami schemed to kill him twice. However, Ōnamuchi was revived by his mother, {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売}}, and fled to the {{ill|Netherworld|ja|根の国}}. After overcoming trials imposed by {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}}, Ōnamuchi married {{ill|Suseribime|ja|須勢理毘売命}} and returned to conquer the Yasogami, becoming the great deity Ōkuninushi. Although Yagami Hime was welcomed to his palace, she feared the jealousy of Suseribime, the principal wife, and fled back to Inaba, leaving her newborn son, {{ill|Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神}}, in a tree fork.
The nearby {{ill|Dake Kofun|ja|嶽古墳}} is considered Yagami Hime’s burial site. Numerous legends about her exist across Japan.
The name "Yagami" likely refers to present-day {{ill|Yazu District|ja|八頭郡}}, and her lack of the suffix "Mikoto" suggests she might have been a priestess of Inaba.<ref>Shinchosha, ''Shincho Nihon Koten Shusei Kojiki''</ref>
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (according to the ''Kojiki''). Blue indicates male deities, red indicates female deities, and yellow indicates unspecified gender.]] Yagami Hime bore one child with Ōkuninushi, {{ill|Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神}}.
== Shrines == * {{ill|Ushinuma Shrine|ja|売沼神社}} ({{ill|Tottori|ja|鳥取県鳥取市河原町}}) * {{ill|Tsunamitada Kamijinja|ja|都波只知上神社}} (Tottori Prefecture) * {{ill|Shimamiko Shrine|ja|島御子神社}} ({{ill|Tsushima|ja|長崎県対馬市}}) * {{ill|Yagami Hime Shrine|ja|八上姫神社}} ({{ill|Izumo|ja|島根県出雲市斐川町}})
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Hare of Inaba|ja|因幡の白兎}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧}} * {{ill|Dake Kofun|ja|嶽古墳}}

The name "Yagami" likely refers to present-day {{ill|Yazu District|ja|八頭郡}}, and her lack of the suffix "Mikoto" suggests she might have been a priestess of Inaba.<ref>Shinchosha, ''Shincho Nihon Koten Shusei Kojiki''</ref>

Blue indicates male deities, red indicates female deities, and yellow indicates unspecified gender.]] Yagami Hime bore one child with Ōkuninushi, {{ill|Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神}}.

{{infobox deity | name = Yagami Hime | predecessor = | successor = | type = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | full name = 八上比売 (Yagami Hime) | other names = 稲羽八上姫 (Inaba Yagami Hime) | alternate = | rank = | role = Unknown | mausoleum = | father = Unknown | mother = | relatives = | consort = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}} | children = {{ill|Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神}} | shrine = {{ill|Ushinuma Shrine|ja|売沼神社}} and others }}
'''Yagami Hime''' (八上比売 / 八上姫, also read as Yagami Hime or Yakami Hime) is a goddess from {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}, associated with {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|因幡国}} in {{ill|Yazu District|ja|八上郡}}. She was the first consort of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}.
== Overview ==
In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, her name is written as '''Yagami Hime''', while in the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, she is referred to as '''Inaba Yagami Hime'''.
Ōnamuchi no Mikoto (Ōkuninushi) traveled to {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|因幡国}} as a servant to his many brothers, the {{ill|Yasogami|ja|八十神}}, who were seeking Yagami Hime’s hand in marriage. Along the way, Ōnamuchi encountered a wounded hare that had been tricked and harmed by the Yasogami. He healed the hare’s injuries, and in return, the hare prophesied that Ōnamuchi would succeed in his courtship—a prediction that proved accurate.<ref>Takeuchi, Tsuneyasu. ''Modern Language Kojiki: Pocket Edition.'' Gakken Plus, 2016. ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6</ref>
Yagami Hime rejected the Yasogami's proposals and chose to marry Ōnamuchi instead. Jealous, the Yasogami schemed to kill him twice. However, Ōnamuchi was revived by his mother, {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売}}, and fled to the {{ill|Netherworld|ja|根の国}}. After overcoming trials imposed by {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}}, Ōnamuchi married {{ill|Suseribime|ja|須勢理毘売命}} and returned to conquer the Yasogami, becoming the great deity Ōkuninushi. Although Yagami Hime was welcomed to his palace, she feared the jealousy of Suseribime, the principal wife, and fled back to Inaba, leaving her newborn son, {{ill|Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神}}, in a tree fork.
The nearby {{ill|Dake Kofun|ja|嶽古墳}} is considered Yagami Hime’s burial site. Numerous legends about her exist across Japan.
The name "Yagami" likely refers to present-day {{ill|Yazu District|ja|八頭郡}}, and her lack of the suffix "Mikoto" suggests she might have been a priestess of Inaba.<ref>Shinchosha, ''Shincho Nihon Koten Shusei Kojiki''</ref>
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (according to the ''Kojiki''). Blue indicates male deities, red indicates female deities, and yellow indicates unspecified gender.]] Yagami Hime bore one child with Ōkuninushi, {{ill|Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神}}.
== Shrines == * {{ill|Ushinuma Shrine|ja|売沼神社}} ({{ill|Tottori|ja|鳥取県鳥取市河原町}}) * {{ill|Tsunamitada Kamijinja|ja|都波只知上神社}} (Tottori Prefecture) * {{ill|Shimamiko Shrine|ja|島御子神社}} ({{ill|Tsushima|ja|長崎県対馬市}}) * {{ill|Yagami Hime Shrine|ja|八上姫神社}} ({{ill|Izumo|ja|島根県出雲市斐川町}})

revision.text:
{{infobox deity | name = Yagami Hime | predecessor = | successor = | type = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | full name = 八上比売 (Yagami Hime) | other names = 稲羽八上姫 (Inaba Yagami Hime) | alternate = | rank = | role = Unknown | mausoleum = | father = Unknown | mother = | relatives = | consort = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}} | children = {{ill|Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神}} | shrine = {{ill|Ushinuma Shrine|ja|売沼神社}} and others }}
'''Yagami Hime''' (八上比売 / 八上姫, also read as Yagami Hime or Yakami Hime) is a goddess from {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}, associated with {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|因幡国}} in {{ill|Yazu District|ja|八上郡}}. She was the first consort of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}.
== Overview ==
In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, her name is written as '''Yagami Hime''', while in the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, she is referred to as '''Inaba Yagami Hime'''.
Ōnamuchi no Mikoto (Ōkuninushi) traveled to {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|因幡国}} as a servant to his many brothers, the {{ill|Yasogami|ja|八十神}}, who were seeking Yagami Hime’s hand in marriage. Along the way, Ōnamuchi encountered a wounded hare that had been tricked and harmed by the Yasogami. He healed the hare’s injuries, and in return, the hare prophesied that Ōnamuchi would succeed in his courtship—a prediction that proved accurate.<ref>Takeuchi, Tsuneyasu. ''Modern Language Kojiki: Pocket Edition.'' Gakken Plus, 2016. ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6</ref>
Yagami Hime rejected the Yasogami's proposals and chose to marry Ōnamuchi instead. Jealous, the Yasogami schemed to kill him twice. However, Ōnamuchi was revived by his mother, {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売}}, and fled to the {{ill|Netherworld|ja|根の国}}. After overcoming trials imposed by {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}}, Ōnamuchi married {{ill|Suseribime|ja|須勢理毘売命}} and returned to conquer the Yasogami, becoming the great deity Ōkuninushi. Although Yagami Hime was welcomed to his palace, she feared the jealousy of Suseribime, the principal wife, and fled back to Inaba, leaving her newborn son, {{ill|Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神}}, in a tree fork.
The nearby {{ill|Dake Kofun|ja|嶽古墳}} is considered Yagami Hime’s burial site. Numerous legends about her exist across Japan.
The name "Yagami" likely refers to present-day {{ill|Yazu District|ja|八頭郡}}, and her lack of the suffix "Mikoto" suggests she might have been a priestess of Inaba.<ref>Shinchosha, ''Shincho Nihon Koten Shusei Kojiki''</ref>
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (according to the ''Kojiki''). Blue indicates male deities, red indicates female deities, and yellow indicates unspecified gender.]] Yagami Hime bore one child with Ōkuninushi, {{ill|Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神}}.
== Shrines == * {{ill|Ushinuma Shrine|ja|売沼神社}} ({{ill|Tottori|ja|鳥取県鳥取市河原町}}) * {{ill|Tsunamitada Kamijinja|ja|都波只知上神社}} (Tottori Prefecture) * {{ill|Shimamiko Shrine|ja|島御子神社}} ({{ill|Tsushima|ja|長崎県対馬市}}) * {{ill|Yagami Hime Shrine|ja|八上姫神社}} ({{ill|Izumo|ja|島根県出雲市斐川町}})
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Hare of Inaba|ja|因幡の白兎}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧}} * {{ill|Dake Kofun|ja|嶽古墳}}

{{infobox deity | name = Yagami Hime | predecessor = | successor = | type = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | full name = 八上比売 (Yagami Hime) | other names = 稲羽八上姫 (Inaba Yagami Hime) | alternate = | rank = | role = Unknown | mausoleum = | father = Unknown | mother = | relatives = | consort = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}} | children = {{ill|Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神}} | shrine = {{ill|Ushinuma Shrine|ja|売沼神社}} and others }}
'''Yagami Hime''' (八上比売 / 八上姫, also read as Yagami Hime or Yakami Hime) is a goddess from {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}, associated with {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|因幡国}} in {{ill|Yazu District|ja|八上郡}}. She was the first consort of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}.
== Overview ==
In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, her name is written as '''Yagami Hime''', while in the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, she is referred to as '''Inaba Yagami Hime'''.
Ōnamuchi no Mikoto (Ōkuninushi) traveled to {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|因幡国}} as a servant to his many brothers, the {{ill|Yasogami|ja|八十神}}, who were seeking Yagami Hime’s hand in marriage. Along the way, Ōnamuchi encountered a wounded hare that had been tricked and harmed by the Yasogami. He healed the hare’s injuries, and in return, the hare prophesied that Ōnamuchi would succeed in his courtship—a prediction that proved accurate.<ref>Takeuchi, Tsuneyasu. ''Modern Language Kojiki: Pocket Edition.'' Gakken Plus, 2016. ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6</ref>
Yagami Hime rejected the Yasogami's proposals and chose to marry Ōnamuchi instead. Jealous, the Yasogami schemed to kill him twice. However, Ōnamuchi was revived by his mother, {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売}}, and fled to the {{ill|Netherworld|ja|根の国}}. After overcoming trials imposed by {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}}, Ōnamuchi married {{ill|Suseribime|ja|須勢理毘売命}} and returned to conquer the Yasogami, becoming the great deity Ōkuninushi. Although Yagami Hime was welcomed to his palace, she feared the jealousy of Suseribime, the principal wife, and fled back to Inaba, leaving her newborn son, {{ill|Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神}}, in a tree fork.
The nearby {{ill|Dake Kofun|ja|嶽古墳}} is considered Yagami Hime’s burial site. Numerous legends about her exist across Japan.
The name "Yagami" likely refers to present-day {{ill|Yazu District|ja|八頭郡}}, and her lack of the suffix "Mikoto" suggests she might have been a priestess of Inaba.<ref>Shinchosha, ''Shincho Nihon Koten Shusei Kojiki''</ref>
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (according to the ''Kojiki''). Blue indicates male deities, red indicates female deities, and yellow indicates unspecified gender.]] Yagami Hime bore one child with Ōkuninushi, {{ill|Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神}}.

After overcoming trials imposed by {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}}, Ōnamuchi married {{ill|Suseribime|ja|須勢理毘売命}} and returned to conquer the Yasogami, becoming the great deity Ōkuninushi. Although Yagami Hime was welcomed to his palace, she feared the jealousy of Suseribime, the principal wife, and fled back to Inaba, leaving her newborn son, {{ill|Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神}}, in a tree fork.
The nearby {{ill|Dake Kofun|ja|嶽古墳}} is considered Yagami Hime’s burial site. Numerous legends about her exist across Japan.
The name "Yagami" likely refers to present-day {{ill|Yazu District|ja|八頭郡}}, and her lack of the suffix "Mikoto" suggests she might have been a priestess of Inaba.<ref>Shinchosha, ''Shincho Nihon Koten Shusei Kojiki''</ref>

'''Yagami Hime''' (八上比売 / 八上姫, also read as Yagami Hime or Yakami Hime) is a goddess from {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}, associated with {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|因幡国}} in {{ill|Yazu District|ja|八上郡}}. She was the first consort of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}.
== Overview ==
In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, her name is written as '''Yagami Hime''', while in the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, she is referred to as '''Inaba Yagami Hime'''.
Ōnamuchi no Mikoto (Ōkuninushi) traveled to {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|因幡国}} as a servant to his many brothers, the {{ill|Yasogami|ja|八十神}}, who were seeking Yagami Hime’s hand in marriage. Along the way, Ōnamuchi encountered a wounded hare that had been tricked and harmed by the Yasogami. He healed the hare’s injuries, and in return, the hare prophesied that Ōnamuchi would succeed in his courtship—a prediction that proved accurate.<ref>Takeuchi, Tsuneyasu. ''Modern Language Kojiki: Pocket Edition.'' Gakken Plus, 2016. ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6</ref>
Yagami Hime rejected the Yasogami's proposals and chose to marry Ōnamuchi instead. Jealous, the Yasogami schemed to kill him twice. However, Ōnamuchi was revived by his mother, {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売}}, and fled to the {{ill|Netherworld|ja|根の国}}. After overcoming trials imposed by {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}}, Ōnamuchi married {{ill|Suseribime|ja|須勢理毘売命}} and returned to conquer the Yasogami, becoming the great deity Ōkuninushi. Although Yagami Hime was welcomed to his palace, she feared the jealousy of Suseribime, the principal wife, and fled back to Inaba, leaving her newborn son, {{ill|Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神}}, in a tree fork.
The nearby {{ill|Dake Kofun|ja|嶽古墳}} is considered Yagami Hime’s burial site. Numerous legends about her exist across Japan.
The name "Yagami" likely refers to present-day {{ill|Yazu District|ja|八頭郡}}, and her lack of the suffix "Mikoto" suggests she might have been a priestess of Inaba.<ref>Shinchosha, ''Shincho Nihon Koten Shusei Kojiki''</ref>
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (according to the ''Kojiki''). Blue indicates male deities, red indicates female deities, and yellow indicates unspecified gender.]] Yagami Hime bore one child with Ōkuninushi, {{ill|Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神}}.

revision.text:
{{infobox deity | name = Yagami Hime | predecessor = | successor = | type = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | full name = 八上比売 (Yagami Hime) | other names = 稲羽八上姫 (Inaba Yagami Hime) | alternate = | rank = | role = Unknown | mausoleum = | father = Unknown | mother = | relatives = | consort = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}} | children = {{ill|Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神}} | shrine = {{ill|Ushinuma Shrine|ja|売沼神社}} and others }}
'''Yagami Hime''' (八上比売 / 八上姫, also read as Yagami Hime or Yakami Hime) is a goddess from {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}, associated with {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|因幡国}} in {{ill|Yazu District|ja|八上郡}}. She was the first consort of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}.
== Overview ==
In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, her name is written as '''Yagami Hime''', while in the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, she is referred to as '''Inaba Yagami Hime'''.
Ōnamuchi no Mikoto (Ōkuninushi) traveled to {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|因幡国}} as a servant to his many brothers, the {{ill|Yasogami|ja|八十神}}, who were seeking Yagami Hime’s hand in marriage. Along the way, Ōnamuchi encountered a wounded hare that had been tricked and harmed by the Yasogami. He healed the hare’s injuries, and in return, the hare prophesied that Ōnamuchi would succeed in his courtship—a prediction that proved accurate.<ref>Takeuchi, Tsuneyasu. ''Modern Language Kojiki: Pocket Edition.'' Gakken Plus, 2016. ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6</ref>
Yagami Hime rejected the Yasogami's proposals and chose to marry Ōnamuchi instead. Jealous, the Yasogami schemed to kill him twice. However, Ōnamuchi was revived by his mother, {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売}}, and fled to the {{ill|Netherworld|ja|根の国}}. After overcoming trials imposed by {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}}, Ōnamuchi married {{ill|Suseribime|ja|須勢理毘売命}} and returned to conquer the Yasogami, becoming the great deity Ōkuninushi. Although Yagami Hime was welcomed to his palace, she feared the jealousy of Suseribime, the principal wife, and fled back to Inaba, leaving her newborn son, {{ill|Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神}}, in a tree fork.
The nearby {{ill|Dake Kofun|ja|嶽古墳}} is considered Yagami Hime’s burial site. Numerous legends about her exist across Japan.
The name "Yagami" likely refers to present-day {{ill|Yazu District|ja|八頭郡}}, and her lack of the suffix "Mikoto" suggests she might have been a priestess of Inaba.<ref>Shinchosha, ''Shincho Nihon Koten Shusei Kojiki''</ref>
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (according to the ''Kojiki''). Blue indicates male deities, red indicates female deities, and yellow indicates unspecified gender.]] Yagami Hime bore one child with Ōkuninushi, {{ill|Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神}}.
== Shrines == * {{ill|Ushinuma Shrine|ja|売沼神社}} ({{ill|Tottori|ja|鳥取県鳥取市河原町}}) * {{ill|Tsunamitada Kamijinja|ja|都波只知上神社}} (Tottori Prefecture) * {{ill|Shimamiko Shrine|ja|島御子神社}} ({{ill|Tsushima|ja|長崎県対馬市}}) * {{ill|Yagami Hime Shrine|ja|八上姫神社}} ({{ill|Izumo|ja|島根県出雲市斐川町}})
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Hare of Inaba|ja|因幡の白兎}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧}} * {{ill|Dake Kofun|ja|嶽古墳}}

{{infobox deity | name = Yagami Hime | predecessor = | successor = | type = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | full name = 八上比売 (Yagami Hime) | other names = 稲羽八上姫 (Inaba Yagami Hime) | alternate = | rank = | role = Unknown | mausoleum = | father = Unknown | mother = | relatives = | consort = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}} | children = {{ill|Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神}} | shrine = {{ill|Ushinuma Shrine|ja|売沼神社}} and others }}
'''Yagami Hime''' (八上比売 / 八上姫, also read as Yagami Hime or Yakami Hime) is a goddess from {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}, associated with {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|因幡国}} in {{ill|Yazu District|ja|八上郡}}. She was the first consort of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}.
== Overview ==
In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, her name is written as '''Yagami Hime''', while in the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, she is referred to as '''Inaba Yagami Hime'''.
Ōnamuchi no Mikoto (Ōkuninushi) traveled to {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|因幡国}} as a servant to his many brothers, the {{ill|Yasogami|ja|八十神}}, who were seeking Yagami Hime’s hand in marriage. Along the way, Ōnamuchi encountered a wounded hare that had been tricked and harmed by the Yasogami. He healed the hare’s injuries, and in return, the hare prophesied that Ōnamuchi would succeed in his courtship—a prediction that proved accurate.<ref>Takeuchi, Tsuneyasu. ''Modern Language Kojiki: Pocket Edition.'' Gakken Plus, 2016. ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6</ref>
Yagami Hime rejected the Yasogami's proposals and chose to marry Ōnamuchi instead. Jealous, the Yasogami schemed to kill him twice. However, Ōnamuchi was revived by his mother, {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売}}, and fled to the {{ill|Netherworld|ja|根の国}}. After overcoming trials imposed by {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}}, Ōnamuchi married {{ill|Suseribime|ja|須勢理毘売命}} and returned to conquer the Yasogami, becoming the great deity Ōkuninushi. Although Yagami Hime was welcomed to his palace, she feared the jealousy of Suseribime, the principal wife, and fled back to Inaba, leaving her newborn son, {{ill|Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神}}, in a tree fork.
The nearby {{ill|Dake Kofun|ja|嶽古墳}} is considered Yagami Hime’s burial site. Numerous legends about her exist across Japan.
The name "Yagami" likely refers to present-day {{ill|Yazu District|ja|八頭郡}}, and her lack of the suffix "Mikoto" suggests she might have been a priestess of Inaba.<ref>Shinchosha, ''Shincho Nihon Koten Shusei Kojiki''</ref>
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (according to the ''Kojiki''). Blue indicates male deities, red indicates female deities, and yellow indicates unspecified gender.]] Yagami Hime bore one child with Ōkuninushi, {{ill|Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神}}.
== Shrines == * {{ill|Ushinuma Shrine|ja|売沼神社}} ({{ill|Tottori|ja|鳥取県鳥取市河原町}}) * {{ill|Tsunamitada Kamijinja|ja|都波只知上神社}} (Tottori Prefecture) * {{ill|Shimamiko Shrine|ja|島御子神社}} ({{ill|Tsushima|ja|長崎県対馬市}}) * {{ill|Yagami Hime Shrine|ja|八上姫神社}} ({{ill|Izumo|ja|島根県出雲市斐川町}})
== Notes == {{reflist}}

{{infobox deity | name = Yagami Hime | predecessor = | successor = | type = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | full name = 八上比売 (Yagami Hime) | other names = 稲羽八上姫 (Inaba Yagami Hime) | alternate = | rank = | role = Unknown | mausoleum = | father = Unknown | mother = | relatives = | consort = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}} | children = {{ill|Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神}} | shrine = {{ill|Ushinuma Shrine|ja|売沼神社}} and others }}
'''Yagami Hime''' (八上比売 / 八上姫, also read as Yagami Hime or Yakami Hime) is a goddess from {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}, associated with {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|因幡国}} in {{ill|Yazu District|ja|八上郡}}. She was the first consort of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}.
== Overview ==
In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, her name is written as '''Yagami Hime''', while in the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, she is referred to as '''Inaba Yagami Hime'''.
Ōnamuchi no Mikoto (Ōkuninushi) traveled to {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|因幡国}} as a servant to his many brothers, the {{ill|Yasogami|ja|八十神}}, who were seeking Yagami Hime’s hand in marriage. Along the way, Ōnamuchi encountered a wounded hare that had been tricked and harmed by the Yasogami. He healed the hare’s injuries, and in return, the hare prophesied that Ōnamuchi would succeed in his courtship—a prediction that proved accurate.<ref>Takeuchi, Tsuneyasu. ''Modern Language Kojiki: Pocket Edition.'' Gakken Plus, 2016. ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6</ref>
Yagami Hime rejected the Yasogami's proposals and chose to marry Ōnamuchi instead. Jealous, the Yasogami schemed to kill him twice. However, Ōnamuchi was revived by his mother, {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売}}, and fled to the {{ill|Ne-no-kuni|ja|根の国|Ne-no-kuni=Ne-no-kuni|lt=Netherworld}}. After overcoming trials imposed by {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}}, Ōnamuchi married {{ill|Suseribime|ja|須勢理毘売命}} and returned to conquer the Yasogami, becoming the great deity Ōkuninushi. Although Yagami Hime was welcomed to his palace, she feared the jealousy of Suseribime, the principal wife, and fled back to Inaba, leaving her newborn son, {{ill|Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神}}, in a tree fork.
The nearby {{ill|Dake Kofun|ja|嶽古墳}} is considered Yagami Hime’s burial site. Numerous legends about her exist across Japan.
The name "Yagami" likely refers to present-day {{ill|Yazu District|ja|八頭郡}}, and her lack of the suffix "Mikoto" suggests she might have been a priestess of Inaba.<ref>Shinchosha, ''Shincho Nihon Koten Shusei Kojiki''</ref>
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (according to the ''Kojiki''). Blue indicates male deities, red indicates female deities, and yellow indicates unspecified gender.]] Yagami Hime bore one child with Ōkuninushi, {{ill|Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神}}.
== Shrines == * {{ill|Ushinuma Shrine|ja|売沼神社}} ({{ill|Tottori|ja|鳥取県鳥取市河原町}}) * {{ill|Tsunamitada Kamijinja|ja|都波只知上神社}} (Tottori Prefecture) * {{ill|Shimamiko Shrine|ja|島御子神社}} ({{ill|Tsushima|ja|長崎県対馬市}}) * {{ill|Yagami Hime Shrine|ja|八上姫神社}} ({{ill|Izumo|ja|島根県出雲市斐川町}})
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Hare of Inaba|ja|因幡の白兎}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧}} * {{ill|Dake Kofun|ja|嶽古墳}}

However, Ōnamuchi was revived by his mother, {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売}}, and fled to the {{ill|Ne-no-kuni|ja|根の国|Ne-no-kuni=Ne-no-kuni|lt=Netherworld}}. After overcoming trials imposed by {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}}, Ōnamuchi married {{ill|Suseribime|ja|須勢理毘売命}} and returned to conquer the Yasogami, becoming the great deity Ōkuninushi. Although Yagami Hime was welcomed to his palace, she feared the jealousy of Suseribime, the principal wife, and fled back to Inaba, leaving her newborn son, {{ill|Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神}}, in a tree fork.
The nearby {{ill|Dake Kofun|ja|嶽古墳}} is considered Yagami Hime’s burial site. Numerous legends about her exist across Japan.
The name "Yagami" likely refers to present-day {{ill|Yazu District|ja|八頭郡}}, and her lack of the suffix "Mikoto" suggests she might have been a priestess of Inaba.<ref>Shinchosha, ''Shincho Nihon Koten Shusei Kojiki''</ref>
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (according to the ''Kojiki''). Blue indicates male deities, red indicates female deities, and yellow indicates unspecified gender.]] Yagami Hime bore one child with Ōkuninushi, {{ill|Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神}}.

{{infobox deity | name = Yagami Hime | predecessor = | successor = | type = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | full name = 八上比売 (Yagami Hime) | other names = 稲羽八上姫 (Inaba Yagami Hime) | alternate = | rank = | role = Unknown | mausoleum = | father = Unknown | mother = | relatives = | consort = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}} | children = {{ill|Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神}} | shrine = {{ill|Ushinuma Shrine|ja|売沼神社}} and others }}
'''Yagami Hime''' (八上比売 / 八上姫, also read as Yagami Hime or Yakami Hime) is a goddess from {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}, associated with {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|因幡国}} in {{ill|Yazu District|ja|八上郡}}. She was the first consort of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}.
== Overview ==
In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, her name is written as '''Yagami Hime''', while in the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, she is referred to as '''Inaba Yagami Hime'''.
Ōnamuchi no Mikoto (Ōkuninushi) traveled to {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|因幡国}} as a servant to his many brothers, the {{ill|Yasogami|ja|八十神}}, who were seeking Yagami Hime’s hand in marriage. Along the way, Ōnamuchi encountered a wounded hare that had been tricked and harmed by the Yasogami. He healed the hare’s injuries, and in return, the hare prophesied that Ōnamuchi would succeed in his courtship—a prediction that proved accurate.<ref>Takeuchi, Tsuneyasu. ''Modern Language Kojiki: Pocket Edition.'' Gakken Plus, 2016. ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6</ref>
Yagami Hime rejected the Yasogami's proposals and chose to marry Ōnamuchi instead. Jealous, the Yasogami schemed to kill him twice. However, Ōnamuchi was revived by his mother, {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売}}, and fled to the {{ill|Ne-no-kuni|ja|根の国|Ne-no-kuni=Ne-no-kuni|lt=Netherworld}}. After overcoming trials imposed by {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}}, Ōnamuchi married {{ill|Suseribime|ja|須勢理毘売命}} and returned to conquer the Yasogami, becoming the great deity Ōkuninushi. Although Yagami Hime was welcomed to his palace, she feared the jealousy of Suseribime, the principal wife, and fled back to Inaba, leaving her newborn son, {{ill|Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神}}, in a tree fork.
The nearby {{ill|Dake Kofun|ja|嶽古墳}} is considered Yagami Hime’s burial site. Numerous legends about her exist across Japan.
The name "Yagami" likely refers to present-day {{ill|Yazu District|ja|八頭郡}}, and her lack of the suffix "Mikoto" suggests she might have been a priestess of Inaba.<ref>Shinchosha, ''Shincho Nihon Koten Shusei Kojiki''</ref>
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (according to the ''Kojiki''). Blue indicates male deities, red indicates female deities, and yellow indicates unspecified gender.]] Yagami Hime bore one child with Ōkuninushi, {{ill|Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神}}.
== Shrines == * {{ill|Ushinuma Shrine|ja|売沼神社}} ({{ill|Tottori|ja|鳥取県鳥取市河原町}}) * {{ill|Tsunamitada Kamijinja|ja|都波只知上神社}} (Tottori Prefecture) * {{ill|Shimamiko Shrine|ja|島御子神社}} ({{ill|Tsushima|ja|長崎県対馬市}}) * {{ill|Yagami Hime Shrine|ja|八上姫神社}} ({{ill|Izumo|ja|島根県出雲市斐川町}})

revision.text:
{{infobox deity | name = Yagami Hime | predecessor = | successor = | type = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | full name = 八上比売 (Yagami Hime) | other names = 稲羽八上姫 (Inaba Yagami Hime) | alternate = | rank = | role = Unknown | mausoleum = | father = Unknown | mother = | relatives = | consort = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}} | children = {{ill|Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神}} | shrine = {{ill|Ushinuma Shrine|ja|売沼神社}} and others }}
'''Yagami Hime''' (八上比売 / 八上姫, also read as Yagami Hime or Yakami Hime) is a goddess from {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}, associated with {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|因幡国}} in {{ill|Yazu District|ja|八上郡}}. She was the first consort of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}.
== Overview ==
In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, her name is written as '''Yagami Hime''', while in the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, she is referred to as '''Inaba Yagami Hime'''.
Ōnamuchi no Mikoto (Ōkuninushi) traveled to {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|因幡国}} as a servant to his many brothers, the {{ill|Yasogami|ja|八十神}}, who were seeking Yagami Hime’s hand in marriage. Along the way, Ōnamuchi encountered a wounded hare that had been tricked and harmed by the Yasogami. He healed the hare’s injuries, and in return, the hare prophesied that Ōnamuchi would succeed in his courtship—a prediction that proved accurate.<ref>Takeuchi, Tsuneyasu. ''Modern Language Kojiki: Pocket Edition.'' Gakken Plus, 2016. ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6</ref>
Yagami Hime rejected the Yasogami's proposals and chose to marry Ōnamuchi instead. Jealous, the Yasogami schemed to kill him twice. However, Ōnamuchi was revived by his mother, {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売}}, and fled to the {{ill|Ne-no-kuni|ja|根の国|Ne-no-kuni=Ne-no-kuni|lt=Netherworld}}. After overcoming trials imposed by {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}}, Ōnamuchi married {{ill|Suseribime|ja|須勢理毘売命}} and returned to conquer the Yasogami, becoming the great deity Ōkuninushi. Although Yagami Hime was welcomed to his palace, she feared the jealousy of Suseribime, the principal wife, and fled back to Inaba, leaving her newborn son, {{ill|Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神}}, in a tree fork.
The nearby {{ill|Dake Kofun|ja|嶽古墳}} is considered Yagami Hime’s burial site. Numerous legends about her exist across Japan.
The name "Yagami" likely refers to present-day {{ill|Yazu District|ja|八頭郡}}, and her lack of the suffix "Mikoto" suggests she might have been a priestess of Inaba.<ref>Shinchosha, ''Shincho Nihon Koten Shusei Kojiki''</ref>
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (according to the ''Kojiki''). Blue indicates male deities, red indicates female deities, and yellow indicates unspecified gender.]] Yagami Hime bore one child with Ōkuninushi, {{ill|Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神}}.
== Shrines == * {{ill|Ushinuma Shrine|ja|売沼神社}} ({{ill|Tottori|ja|鳥取県鳥取市河原町}}) * {{ill|Tsunamitada Kamijinja|ja|都波只知上神社}} (Tottori Prefecture) * {{ill|Shimamiko Shrine|ja|島御子神社}} ({{ill|Tsushima|ja|長崎県対馬市}}) * {{ill|Yagami Hime Shrine|ja|八上姫神社}} ({{ill|Izumo|ja|島根県出雲市斐川町}})
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Hare of Inaba|ja|因幡の白兎}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧}} * {{ill|Dake Kofun|ja|嶽古墳}}

revision.text:
{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 八上比売 | 先代 = | 次代 = | 神祇 = <!-- 天神、地祇、天津神、国津神など -->[[国津神]] | 全名 = 八上比売 | 別名 = 稲羽八上姫 | 別称 = | 神階 = | 神格 = <!-- 太陽、月、山、海など司るもの -->不明 | 陵所 = | 父 = 不明 | 母 = | 親 = <!-- まぐあいによって生まれない場合 --> | 配偶者 = [[大国主神]] | 子 = [[木俣神]] | 宮 = | 神社 = [[売沼神社]]等 }}
'''八上比売'''（やがみひめ / やかみひめ、'''八上姫'''）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[因幡国]][[八上郡 (鳥取県)|八上郡]]の[[女神]]。[[大国主神]]の最初の[[妃]]。
== 概要 == 『[[古事記]]』において'''八上比売'''、『[[先代旧事本紀]]』では'''稲羽八上姫'''と表記されている。
[[大穴牟遅神]]の大勢の兄弟神（[[八十神]]）は大穴牟遅神を荷物持ちとして、八上比売への求婚のために[[因幡国]]へと向かった。その途中、負傷した挙げ句八十神たちに騙されて、傷が痛んだ兎に大穴牟遅神は出会いこれを救う（[[因幡の白兎]]）。救われた白兎は「あなたの求婚は成功するでしょう」という予言を残し、この予言は的中する<ref>[[竹田恒泰]]『現代語古事記 ポケット版』[[学研プラス]]、2016年。ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6</ref>。
八上比売は八十神たちの求婚をはね除け、大穴牟遅神との結婚を宣言するが、妬んだ八十神たちの謀略によって大穴牟遅神は二度殺されてしまう。母の[[刺国若比売]]などに救われた大穴牟遅神は[[根の国|根之堅洲国]]に逃れ、そこで受けた[[須佐之男命]]の試練を乗り越え、[[須勢理毘売命]]を娶り、大国主神となって八十神たちを平定する。その後大国主神は八上比売を宮殿に迎え入れるが、正妻となった須勢理毘売命の嫉妬を恐れ、生まれたばかりの子を木の俣に挟んで因幡の国へ帰ってしまう。
売沼神社に近い[[嶽古墳]]が八上比売の神陵とされる他、各地に八上比売の関連伝承が存在する。
「八上」は現在の「[[八頭郡]]」、「命」が着かないことは巫女性を表すと解し、名義は「因幡国八上郡の豪族の娘である巫女」と考えられる<ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref>。
== 系譜 == [[画像:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|大国主の系図（『古事記』による）。青は男神、赤は女神、黄は性別不詳]] [[大国主神]]との間に[[木俣神]]を生んでいる。
== 祀る神社 == * [[売沼神社]]（[[鳥取県]][[鳥取市]][[河原町 (鳥取県)|河原町]]） * [[都波只知上神社]]（鳥取県鳥取市河原町） * [[島御子神社]]（[[長崎県]][[対馬市]][[豊玉町]]） * [[八上姫神社]]（[[島根県]][[出雲市]][[斐川町]]）
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[因幡の白兎]] * [[日本の神の一覧]] * [[嶽古墳]]
{{日本神話}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やかみひめ}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:女神]] [[Category:国津神]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:八上比売]][[zh:八上比賣]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|qid=Q6581072}}|spouse={{ill|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944}}|children={{ill|Ki no mata no kami|qid=Q11092833}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q10891903]] Wikidata name: Ya kami hime [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox deity | name = Yagami Hime | predecessor = | successor = | type = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | full name = 八上比売 (Yagami Hime) | other names = 稲羽八上姫 (Inaba Yagami Hime) | alternate = | Rank = | role = Unknown | mausoleum = | father = Unknown | mother = | relatives = | consort ={{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=大国主}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944}}| children = {{ill|Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimata no Kami|qq=}} | shrine = {{ill|Ushinuma Shrine|ja|売沼神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushinuma Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and others | gender= {{ill|female|qid=Q6581072}} | offspring= {{ill|Ki no mata no kami|qid=Q11092833}} }}
'''Yagami Hime''' (八上比売 / 八上姫, also read as Yagami Hime or Yakami Hime) is a goddess from [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]], associated with {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Yazu District|ja|八上郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yazu District|qq=|1=Yazu District|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10891904}}. She was the first consort of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=大国主}}.
== Overview ==
In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], her name is written as '''Yagami Hime''', while in the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], she is referred to as '''Inaba Yagami Hime'''.
Ōnamuchi no Mikoto (Ōkuninushi) traveled to {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a servant to his many brothers, the {{ill|Yasogami|ja|八十神|1=Yasogami|lt=Yasogami (80 gods)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}, who were seeking Yagami Hime’s hand in marriage. Along the way, Ōnamuchi encountered a wounded hare that had been tricked and harmed by the Yasogami. He healed the hare’s injuries, and in return, the hare prophesied that Ōnamuchi would succeed in his courtship—a prediction that proved accurate.<ref>Takeuchi, Tsuneyasu. ''Modern Language Kojiki: Pocket Edition.'' Gakken Plus, 2016. ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6</ref>
Yagami Hime rejected the Yasogami's proposals and chose to marry Ōnamuchi instead. Jealous, the Yasogami schemed to kill him twice. However, Ōnamuchi was revived by his mother, {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}}, and fled to the [[:en:Ne-no-kuni|Netherworld]]. After overcoming trials imposed by {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}}, Ōnamuchi married {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime|ja|須勢理毘売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseribime|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スセリビメ}} and returned to conquer the Yasogami, becoming the great deity Ōkuninushi. Although Yagami Hime was welcomed to his palace, she feared the jealousy of Suseribime, the principal wife, and fled back to Inaba, leaving her newborn son, {{ill|Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimata no Kami|qq=}}, in a tree fork.
The nearby {{ill|Dake Kofun|ja|嶽古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dake Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is considered Yagami Hime’s burial site. Numerous legends about her exist across Japan.
The name "Yagami" likely refers to present-day [[:en:Yazu District, Tottori|Yazu District]], and her lack of the suffix "Mikoto" suggests she might have been a priestess of Inaba.<ref>Shinchosha, ''Shincho Nihon Koten Shusei Kojiki''</ref>
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (according to the ''Kojiki''). Blue indicates male deities, red indicates female deities, and yellow indicates unspecified gender.]] Yagami Hime bore one child with Ōkuninushi, {{ill|Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimata no Kami|qq=}}.
== Shrines == * {{ill|Ushinuma Shrine|ja|売沼神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushinuma Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Tottori|ja|鳥取県鳥取市河原町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * {{ill|Tsunamitada Kamijinja|ja|都波只知上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunamitada Kamijinja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tottori Prefecture) * {{ill|Shimamiko Shrine|ja|島御子神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimamiko Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Tsushima|ja|長崎県対馬市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * {{ill|Yagami Hime Shrine|ja|八上姫神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagami Hime Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Izumo|ja|島根県出雲市斐川町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}})
== Notes == {{reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Hare of Inaba|Hare of Inaba]] * [[:en:List of Japanese deities|List of Japanese deities]] * {{ill|Dake Kofun|ja|嶽古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dake Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

'''Yagami Hime''' (八上比売 / 八上姫, also read as Yagami Hime or Yakami Hime) is a goddess from [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]], associated with {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Yazu District|ja|八上郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yazu District|qq=|1=Yazu District|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10891904}}. She was the first consort of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=大国主}}.
== Overview ==
In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], her name is written as '''Yagami Hime''', while in the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], she is referred to as '''Inaba Yagami Hime'''.
Ōnamuchi no Mikoto (Ōkuninushi) traveled to {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a servant to his many brothers, the {{ill|Yasogami|ja|八十神|1=Yasogami|lt=Yasogami (80 gods)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}, who were seeking Yagami Hime’s hand in marriage. Along the way, Ōnamuchi encountered a wounded hare that had been tricked and harmed by the Yasogami. He healed the hare’s injuries, and in return, the hare prophesied that Ōnamuchi would succeed in his courtship—a prediction that proved accurate.<ref>Takeuchi, Tsuneyasu. ''Modern Language Kojiki: Pocket Edition.'' Gakken Plus, 2016. ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6</ref>
Yagami Hime rejected the Yasogami's proposals and chose to marry Ōnamuchi instead. Jealous, the Yasogami schemed to kill him twice. However, Ōnamuchi was revived by his mother, {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}}, and fled to the [[:en:Ne-no-kuni|Netherworld]]. After overcoming trials imposed by {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}}, Ōnamuchi married {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime|ja|須勢理毘売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseribime|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スセリビメ}} and returned to conquer the Yasogami, becoming the great deity Ōkuninushi. Although Yagami Hime was welcomed to his palace, she feared the jealousy of Suseribime, the principal wife, and fled back to Inaba, leaving her newborn son, {{ill|Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimata no Kami|qq=}}, in a tree fork.
The nearby {{ill|Dake Kofun|ja|嶽古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dake Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is considered Yagami Hime’s burial site. Numerous legends about her exist across Japan.
The name "Yagami" likely refers to present-day [[:en:Yazu District, Tottori|Yazu District]], and her lack of the suffix "Mikoto" suggests she might have been a priestess of Inaba.<ref>Shinchosha, ''Shincho Nihon Koten Shusei Kojiki''</ref>
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (according to the ''Kojiki''). Blue indicates male deities, red indicates female deities, and yellow indicates unspecified gender.]] Yagami Hime bore one child with Ōkuninushi, {{ill|Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimata no Kami|qq=}}.
== Shrines == * {{ill|Ushinuma Shrine|ja|売沼神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushinuma Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Tottori|ja|鳥取県鳥取市河原町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * {{ill|Tsunamitada Kamijinja|ja|都波只知上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunamitada Kamijinja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tottori Prefecture) * {{ill|Shimamiko Shrine|ja|島御子神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimamiko Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Tsushima|ja|長崎県対馬市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * {{ill|Yagami Hime Shrine|ja|八上姫神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagami Hime Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Izumo|ja|島根県出雲市斐川町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}})

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:八上比売]][[zh:八上比賣]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|spouse={{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}|children={{ill|Ki no mata no kami|WD=Q11092833}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q10891903]] Wikidata name: Ya kami hime [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox deity | name = Yagami Hime | predecessor = | successor = | type = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | full name = 八上比売 (Yagami Hime) | other names = 稲羽八上姫 (Inaba Yagami Hime) | alternate = | Rank = | role = Unknown | mausoleum = | father = Unknown | mother = | relatives = | consort ={{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=大国主}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}| children = {{ill|Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimata no Kami|qq=}} | shrine = {{ill|Ushinuma Shrine|ja|売沼神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushinuma Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and others | gender= {{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}} | offspring= {{ill|Ki no mata no kami|WD=Q11092833}} }}
'''Yagami Hime''' (八上比売 / 八上姫, also read as Yagami Hime or Yakami Hime) is a goddess from [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]], associated with {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Yazu District|ja|八上郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yazu District|1=Yazu District|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10891904}}. She was the first consort of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=大国主}}.
== Overview ==
In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], her name is written as '''Yagami Hime''', while in the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], she is referred to as '''Inaba Yagami Hime'''.
Ōnamuchi no Mikoto (Ōkuninushi) traveled to {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a servant to his many brothers, the {{ill|Yasogami|ja|八十神|1=Yasogami|lt=Yasogami (80 gods)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}, who were seeking Yagami Hime’s hand in marriage. Along the way, Ōnamuchi encountered a wounded hare that had been tricked and harmed by the Yasogami. He healed the hare’s injuries, and in return, the hare prophesied that Ōnamuchi would succeed in his courtship—a prediction that proved accurate.<ref>Takeuchi, Tsuneyasu. ''Modern Language Kojiki: Pocket Edition.'' Gakken Plus, 2016. ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6</ref>
Yagami Hime rejected the Yasogami's proposals and chose to marry Ōnamuchi instead. Jealous, the Yasogami schemed to kill him twice. However, Ōnamuchi was revived by his mother, {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}}, and fled to the [[:en:Ne-no-kuni|Netherworld]]. After overcoming trials imposed by {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}}, Ōnamuchi married {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime|ja|須勢理毘売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseribime|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スセリビメ}} and returned to conquer the Yasogami, becoming the great deity Ōkuninushi. Although Yagami Hime was welcomed to his palace, she feared the jealousy of Suseribime, the principal wife, and fled back to Inaba, leaving her newborn son, {{ill|Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimata no Kami|qq=}}, in a tree fork.
The nearby {{ill|Dake Kofun|ja|嶽古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dake Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is considered Yagami Hime’s burial site. Numerous legends about her exist across Japan.
The name "Yagami" likely refers to present-day [[:en:Yazu District, Tottori|Yazu District]], and her lack of the suffix "Mikoto" suggests she might have been a priestess of Inaba.<ref>Shinchosha, ''Shincho Nihon Koten Shusei Kojiki''</ref>
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (according to the ''Kojiki''). Blue indicates male deities, red indicates female deities, and yellow indicates unspecified gender.]] Yagami Hime bore one child with Ōkuninushi, {{ill|Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimata no Kami|qq=}}.
== Shrines == * {{ill|Ushinuma Shrine|ja|売沼神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushinuma Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Tottori|ja|鳥取県鳥取市河原町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * {{ill|Tsunamitada Kamijinja|ja|都波只知上神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunamitada Kamijinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tottori Prefecture) * {{ill|Shimamiko Shrine|ja|島御子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimamiko Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Tsushima|ja|長崎県対馬市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * {{ill|Yagami Hime Shrine|ja|八上姫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagami Hime Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Izumo|ja|島根県出雲市斐川町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}})
== Notes == {{reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Hare of Inaba|Hare of Inaba]] * [[:en:List of Japanese deities|List of Japanese deities]] * {{ill|Dake Kofun|ja|嶽古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dake Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

'''Yagami Hime''' (八上比売 / 八上姫, also read as Yagami Hime or Yakami Hime) is a goddess from [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]], associated with {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Yazu District|ja|八上郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yazu District|1=Yazu District|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10891904}}. She was the first consort of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=大国主}}.
== Overview ==
In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], her name is written as '''Yagami Hime''', while in the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], she is referred to as '''Inaba Yagami Hime'''.
Ōnamuchi no Mikoto (Ōkuninushi) traveled to {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a servant to his many brothers, the {{ill|Yasogami|ja|八十神|1=Yasogami|lt=Yasogami (80 gods)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}, who were seeking Yagami Hime’s hand in marriage. Along the way, Ōnamuchi encountered a wounded hare that had been tricked and harmed by the Yasogami. He healed the hare’s injuries, and in return, the hare prophesied that Ōnamuchi would succeed in his courtship—a prediction that proved accurate.<ref>Takeuchi, Tsuneyasu. ''Modern Language Kojiki: Pocket Edition.'' Gakken Plus, 2016. ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6</ref>
Yagami Hime rejected the Yasogami's proposals and chose to marry Ōnamuchi instead. Jealous, the Yasogami schemed to kill him twice. However, Ōnamuchi was revived by his mother, {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}}, and fled to the [[:en:Ne-no-kuni|Netherworld]]. After overcoming trials imposed by {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}}, Ōnamuchi married {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime|ja|須勢理毘売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseribime|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スセリビメ}} and returned to conquer the Yasogami, becoming the great deity Ōkuninushi. Although Yagami Hime was welcomed to his palace, she feared the jealousy of Suseribime, the principal wife, and fled back to Inaba, leaving her newborn son, {{ill|Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimata no Kami|qq=}}, in a tree fork.
The nearby {{ill|Dake Kofun|ja|嶽古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dake Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is considered Yagami Hime’s burial site. Numerous legends about her exist across Japan.
The name "Yagami" likely refers to present-day [[:en:Yazu District, Tottori|Yazu District]], and her lack of the suffix "Mikoto" suggests she might have been a priestess of Inaba.<ref>Shinchosha, ''Shincho Nihon Koten Shusei Kojiki''</ref>
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (according to the ''Kojiki''). Blue indicates male deities, red indicates female deities, and yellow indicates unspecified gender.]] Yagami Hime bore one child with Ōkuninushi, {{ill|Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimata no Kami|qq=}}.
== Shrines == * {{ill|Ushinuma Shrine|ja|売沼神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushinuma Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Tottori|ja|鳥取県鳥取市河原町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * {{ill|Tsunamitada Kamijinja|ja|都波只知上神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunamitada Kamijinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tottori Prefecture) * {{ill|Shimamiko Shrine|ja|島御子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimamiko Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Tsushima|ja|長崎県対馬市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * {{ill|Yagami Hime Shrine|ja|八上姫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagami Hime Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Izumo|ja|島根県出雲市斐川町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}})
== Notes == {{reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Hare of Inaba|Hare of Inaba]] * [[:en:List of Japanese deities|List of Japanese deities]] * {{ill|Dake Kofun|ja|嶽古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dake Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:八上比売]][[zh:八上比賣]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|spouse={{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}|children={{ill|Ki no mata no kami|WD=Q11092833}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q10891903]] Wikidata name: Ya kami hime [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox deity | name = Yagami Hime | predecessor = | successor = | type = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | full name = 八上比売 (Yagami Hime) | other names = 稲羽八上姫 (Inaba Yagami Hime) | alternate = | Rank = | role = Unknown | mausoleum = | father = Unknown | mother = | relatives = | consort ={{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=大国主}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}| children = {{ill|Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimata no Kami|qq=}} | shrine = {{ill|Ushinuma Shrine|ja|売沼神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushinuma Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and others | gender= {{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}} | offspring= {{ill|Ki no mata no kami|WD=Q11092833}} }}
'''Yagami Hime''' (八上比売 / 八上姫, also read as Yagami Hime or Yakami Hime) is a goddess from [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]], associated with {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Yazu District|ja|八上郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yazu District|1=Yazu District|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10891904}}. She was the first consort of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=大国主}}.
== Overview ==
In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], her name is written as '''Yagami Hime''', while in the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], she is referred to as '''Inaba Yagami Hime'''.
Ōnamuchi no Mikoto (Ōkuninushi) traveled to {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a servant to his many brothers, the {{ill|Yasogami|ja|八十神|1=Yasogami|lt=Yasogami (80 gods)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}, who were seeking Yagami Hime’s hand in marriage. Along the way, Ōnamuchi encountered a wounded hare that had been tricked and harmed by the Yasogami. He healed the hare’s injuries, and in return, the hare prophesied that Ōnamuchi would succeed in his courtship—a prediction that proved accurate.<ref>Takeuchi, Tsuneyasu. ''Modern Language Kojiki: Pocket Edition.'' Gakken Plus, 2016. ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6</ref>
Yagami Hime rejected the Yasogami's proposals and chose to marry Ōnamuchi instead. Jealous, the Yasogami schemed to kill him twice. However, Ōnamuchi was revived by his mother, {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}}, and fled to the [[:en:Ne-no-kuni|Netherworld]]. After overcoming trials imposed by {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}}, Ōnamuchi married {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime|ja|須勢理毘売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseribime|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スセリビメ}} and returned to conquer the Yasogami, becoming the great deity Ōkuninushi. Although Yagami Hime was welcomed to his palace, she feared the jealousy of Suseribime, the principal wife, and fled back to Inaba, leaving her newborn son, {{ill|Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimata no Kami|qq=}}, in a tree fork.
The nearby {{ill|Dake Kofun|ja|嶽古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dake Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is considered Yagami Hime’s burial site. Numerous legends about her exist across Japan.
The name "Yagami" likely refers to present-day [[:en:Yazu District, Tottori|Yazu District]], and her lack of the suffix "Mikoto" suggests she might have been a priestess of Inaba.<ref>Shinchosha, ''Shincho Nihon Koten Shusei Kojiki''</ref>
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (according to the ''Kojiki''). Blue indicates male deities, red indicates female deities, and yellow indicates unspecified gender.]] Yagami Hime bore one child with Ōkuninushi, {{ill|Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimata no Kami|qq=}}.
== Shrines == * {{ill|Ushinuma Shrine|ja|売沼神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushinuma Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Tottori|ja|鳥取県鳥取市河原町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * {{ill|Tsunamitada Kamijinja|ja|都波只知上神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunamitada Kamijinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tottori Prefecture) * {{ill|Shimamiko Shrine|ja|島御子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimamiko Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Tsushima|ja|長崎県対馬市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * {{ill|Yagami Hime Shrine|ja|八上姫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagami Hime Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Izumo|ja|島根県出雲市斐川町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}})
== Notes == {{reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Hare of Inaba|Hare of Inaba]] * [[:en:List of Japanese deities|List of Japanese deities]] * {{ill|Dake Kofun|ja|嶽古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dake Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:八上比売]][[zh:八上比賣]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|spouse={{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}|children={{ill|Ki no mata no kami|WD=Q11092833}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q10891903]] Wikidata name: Ya kami hime [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox deity | name = Yagami Hime | predecessor = | successor = | type = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | full name = 八上比売 (Yagami Hime) | other names = 稲羽八上姫 (Inaba Yagami Hime) | alternate = | Rank = | role = Unknown | mausoleum = | father = Unknown | mother = | relatives = | consort ={{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=大国主}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}| children = {{ill|Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimata no Kami|qq=}} | shrine = {{ill|Ushinuma Shrine|ja|売沼神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushinuma Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and others | gender= {{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}} | offspring= {{ill|Ki no mata no kami|WD=Q11092833}} }}
'''Yagami Hime''' (八上比売 / 八上姫, also read as Yagami Hime or Yakami Hime) is a goddess from [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]], associated with {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Yazu District|ja|八上郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yazu District|1=Yazu District|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10891904}}. She was the first consort of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=大国主}}.
== Overview ==
In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], her name is written as '''Yagami Hime''', while in the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], she is referred to as '''Inaba Yagami Hime'''.
Ōnamuchi no Mikoto (Ōkuninushi) traveled to {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a servant to his many brothers, the {{ill|Yasogami|ja|八十神|1=Yasogami|lt=Yasogami (80 gods)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}, who were seeking Yagami Hime’s hand in marriage. Along the way, Ōnamuchi encountered a wounded hare that had been tricked and harmed by the Yasogami. He healed the hare’s injuries, and in return, the hare prophesied that Ōnamuchi would succeed in his courtship—a prediction that proved accurate.<ref>Takeuchi, Tsuneyasu. ''Modern Language Kojiki: Pocket Edition.'' Gakken Plus, 2016. ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6</ref>
Yagami Hime rejected the Yasogami's proposals and chose to marry Ōnamuchi instead. Jealous, the Yasogami schemed to kill him twice. However, Ōnamuchi was revived by his mother, {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}}, and fled to the [[:en:Ne-no-kuni|Netherworld]]. After overcoming trials imposed by {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}}, Ōnamuchi married {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime|ja|須勢理毘売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseribime|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スセリビメ}} and returned to conquer the Yasogami, becoming the great deity Ōkuninushi. Although Yagami Hime was welcomed to his palace, she feared the jealousy of Suseribime, the principal wife, and fled back to Inaba, leaving her newborn son, {{ill|Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimata no Kami|qq=}}, in a tree fork.
The nearby {{ill|Dake Kofun|ja|嶽古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dake Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is considered Yagami Hime’s burial site. Numerous legends about her exist across Japan.
The name "Yagami" likely refers to present-day [[:en:Yazu District, Tottori|Yazu District]], and her lack of the suffix "Mikoto" suggests she might have been a priestess of Inaba.<ref>Shinchosha, ''Shincho Nihon Koten Shusei Kojiki''</ref>
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (according to the ''Kojiki''). Blue indicates male deities, red indicates female deities, and yellow indicates unspecified gender.]] Yagami Hime bore one child with Ōkuninushi, {{ill|Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimata no Kami|qq=}}.
== Shrines == * {{ill|Ushinuma Shrine|ja|売沼神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushinuma Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Tottori|ja|鳥取県鳥取市河原町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * {{ill|Tsunamitada Kamijinja|ja|都波只知上神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunamitada Kamijinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tottori Prefecture) * {{ill|Shimamiko Shrine|ja|島御子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimamiko Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Tsushima|ja|長崎県対馬市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * {{ill|Yagami Hime Shrine|ja|八上姫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagami Hime Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Izumo|ja|島根県出雲市斐川町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}})
== Notes == {{reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Hare of Inaba|Hare of Inaba]] * [[:en:List of Japanese deities|List of Japanese deities]] * {{ill|Dake Kofun|ja|嶽古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dake Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
