revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:八上比売]][[zh:八上比賣]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:八上比売]][[zh:八上比賣]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:八上比売]][[zh:八上比賣]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|spouse={{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}|children={{ill|Ki no mata no kami|WD=Q11092833}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q10891903]] Wikidata name: Ya kami hime [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox deity | name = Yagami Hime | predecessor = | successor = | type = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | full name = 八上比売 (Yagami Hime) | other names = 稲羽八上姫 (Inaba Yagami Hime) | alternate = | Rank = | role = Unknown | mausoleum = | father = Unknown | mother = | relatives = | consort ={{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=大国主}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}| children = {{ill|Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimata no Kami|qq=}} | shrine = {{ill|Ushinuma Shrine|ja|売沼神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushinuma Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and others | gender= {{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}} | offspring= {{ill|Ki no mata no kami|WD=Q11092833}} }}
'''Yagami Hime''' (八上比売 / 八上姫, also read as Yagami Hime or Yakami Hime) is a goddess from [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]], associated with {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Yazu District|ja|八上郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yazu District|1=Yazu District|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10891904}}. She was the first consort of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=大国主}}.
== Overview ==
In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], her name is written as '''Yagami Hime''', while in the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], she is referred to as '''Inaba Yagami Hime'''.
Ōnamuchi no Mikoto (Ōkuninushi) traveled to {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a servant to his many brothers, the {{ill|Yasogami|ja|八十神|1=Yasogami|lt=Yasogami (80 gods)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}, who were seeking Yagami Hime’s hand in marriage. Along the way, Ōnamuchi encountered a wounded hare that had been tricked and harmed by the Yasogami. He healed the hare’s injuries, and in return, the hare prophesied that Ōnamuchi would succeed in his courtship—a prediction that proved accurate.<ref>Takeuchi, Tsuneyasu. ''Modern Language Kojiki: Pocket Edition.'' Gakken Plus, 2016. ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6</ref>
Yagami Hime rejected the Yasogami's proposals and chose to marry Ōnamuchi instead. Jealous, the Yasogami schemed to kill him twice. However, Ōnamuchi was revived by his mother, {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}}, and fled to the [[:en:Ne-no-kuni|Netherworld]]. After overcoming trials imposed by {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}}, Ōnamuchi married {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime|ja|須勢理毘売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseribime|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スセリビメ}} and returned to conquer the Yasogami, becoming the great deity Ōkuninushi. Although Yagami Hime was welcomed to his palace, she feared the jealousy of Suseribime, the principal wife, and fled back to Inaba, leaving her newborn son, {{ill|Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimata no Kami|qq=}}, in a tree fork.
The nearby {{ill|Dake Kofun|ja|嶽古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dake Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is considered Yagami Hime’s burial site. Numerous legends about her exist across Japan.
The name "Yagami" likely refers to present-day [[:en:Yazu District, Tottori|Yazu District]], and her lack of the suffix "Mikoto" suggests she might have been a priestess of Inaba.<ref>Shinchosha, ''Shincho Nihon Koten Shusei Kojiki''</ref>
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (according to the ''Kojiki''). Blue indicates male deities, red indicates female deities, and yellow indicates unspecified gender.]] Yagami Hime bore one child with Ōkuninushi, {{ill|Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimata no Kami|qq=}}.
== Shrines == * {{ill|Ushinuma Shrine|ja|売沼神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushinuma Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Tottori|ja|鳥取県鳥取市河原町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * {{ill|Tsunamitada Kamijinja|ja|都波只知上神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunamitada Kamijinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tottori Prefecture) * {{ill|Shimamiko Shrine|ja|島御子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimamiko Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Tsushima|ja|長崎県対馬市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * {{ill|Yagami Hime Shrine|ja|八上姫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagami Hime Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Izumo|ja|島根県出雲市斐川町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}})
== Notes == {{reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Hare of Inaba|Hare of Inaba]] * [[:en:List of Japanese deities|List of Japanese deities]] * {{ill|Dake Kofun|ja|嶽古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dake Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:八上比売]][[zh:八上比賣]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:八上比売]][[zh:八上比賣]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:八上比売]][[zh:八上比賣]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|spouse={{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}|children={{ill|Ki no mata no kami|WD=Q11092833}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q10891903]] Wikidata name: Ya kami hime [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox deity | name = Yagami Hime | predecessor = | successor = | type = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | full name = 八上比売 (Yagami Hime) | other names = 稲羽八上姫 (Inaba Yagami Hime) | alternate = | Rank = | role = Unknown | mausoleum = | father = Unknown | mother = | relatives = | consort ={{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=大国主}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}| children = {{ill|Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimata no Kami|qq=}} | shrine = {{ill|Ushinuma Shrine|ja|売沼神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushinuma Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and others | gender= {{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}} | offspring= {{ill|Ki no mata no kami|WD=Q11092833}} }}
'''Yagami Hime''' (八上比売 / 八上姫, also read as Yagami Hime or Yakami Hime) is a goddess from [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]], associated with {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Yazu District|ja|八上郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yazu District|1=Yazu District|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10891904}}. She was the first consort of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=大国主}}.
== Overview ==
In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], her name is written as '''Yagami Hime''', while in the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], she is referred to as '''Inaba Yagami Hime'''.
Ōnamuchi no Mikoto (Ōkuninushi) traveled to {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a servant to his many brothers, the {{ill|Yasogami|ja|八十神|1=Yasogami|lt=Yasogami (80 gods)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}, who were seeking Yagami Hime’s hand in marriage. Along the way, Ōnamuchi encountered a wounded hare that had been tricked and harmed by the Yasogami. He healed the hare’s injuries, and in return, the hare prophesied that Ōnamuchi would succeed in his courtship—a prediction that proved accurate.<ref>Takeuchi, Tsuneyasu. ''Modern Language Kojiki: Pocket Edition.'' Gakken Plus, 2016. ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6</ref>
Yagami Hime rejected the Yasogami's proposals and chose to marry Ōnamuchi instead. Jealous, the Yasogami schemed to kill him twice. However, Ōnamuchi was revived by his mother, {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}}, and fled to the [[:en:Ne-no-kuni|Netherworld]]. After overcoming trials imposed by {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}}, Ōnamuchi married {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime|ja|須勢理毘売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseribime|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スセリビメ}} and returned to conquer the Yasogami, becoming the great deity Ōkuninushi. Although Yagami Hime was welcomed to his palace, she feared the jealousy of Suseribime, the principal wife, and fled back to Inaba, leaving her newborn son, {{ill|Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimata no Kami|qq=}}, in a tree fork.
The nearby {{ill|Dake Kofun|ja|嶽古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dake Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is considered Yagami Hime’s burial site. Numerous legends about her exist across Japan.
The name "Yagami" likely refers to present-day [[:en:Yazu District, Tottori|Yazu District]], and her lack of the suffix "Mikoto" suggests she might have been a priestess of Inaba.<ref>Shinchosha, ''Shincho Nihon Koten Shusei Kojiki''</ref>
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (according to the ''Kojiki''). Blue indicates male deities, red indicates female deities, and yellow indicates unspecified gender.]] Yagami Hime bore one child with Ōkuninushi, {{ill|Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimata no Kami|qq=}}.
== Shrines == * {{ill|Ushinuma Shrine|ja|売沼神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushinuma Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Tottori|ja|鳥取県鳥取市河原町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * {{ill|Tsunamitada Kamijinja|ja|都波只知上神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunamitada Kamijinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tottori Prefecture) * {{ill|Shimamiko Shrine|ja|島御子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimamiko Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Tsushima|ja|長崎県対馬市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * {{ill|Yagami Hime Shrine|ja|八上姫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagami Hime Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Izumo|ja|島根県出雲市斐川町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}})
== Notes == {{reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Hare of Inaba|Hare of Inaba]] * [[:en:List of Japanese deities|List of Japanese deities]] * {{ill|Dake Kofun|ja|嶽古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dake Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:八上比売]][[zh:八上比賣]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:八上比売]][[zh:八上比賣]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:八上比売]][[zh:八上比賣]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|spouse={{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}|children={{ill|Ki no mata no kami|WD=Q11092833}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q10891903]] Wikidata name: Ya kami hime [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox deity | name = Yagami Hime | predecessor = | successor = | type = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | full name = 八上比売 (Yagami Hime) | other names = 稲羽八上姫 (Inaba Yagami Hime) | alternate = | Rank = | role = Unknown | mausoleum = | father = Unknown | mother = | relatives = | consort ={{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=大国主}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}| children = {{ill|Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimata no Kami|qq=}} | shrine = {{ill|Ushinuma Shrine|ja|売沼神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushinuma Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and others | gender= {{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}} | offspring= {{ill|Ki no mata no kami|WD=Q11092833}} }}
'''Yagami Hime''' (八上比売 / 八上姫, also read as Yagami Hime or Yakami Hime) is a goddess from {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology}}, associated with {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Yazu District|ja|八上郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yazu District|1=Yazu District|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10891904}}. She was the first consort of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=大国主}}.
== Overview ==
In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, her name is written as '''Yagami Hime''', while in the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}}, she is referred to as '''Inaba Yagami Hime'''.
Ōnamuchi no Mikoto (Ōkuninushi) traveled to {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a servant to his many brothers, the {{ill|Yasogami|ja|八十神|1=Yasogami|lt=Yasogami (80 gods)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}, who were seeking Yagami Hime’s hand in marriage. Along the way, Ōnamuchi encountered a wounded hare that had been tricked and harmed by the Yasogami. He healed the hare’s injuries, and in return, the hare prophesied that Ōnamuchi would succeed in his courtship—a prediction that proved accurate.<ref>Takeuchi, Tsuneyasu. ''Modern Language Kojiki: Pocket Edition.'' Gakken Plus, 2016. ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6</ref>
Yagami Hime rejected the Yasogami's proposals and chose to marry Ōnamuchi instead. Jealous, the Yasogami schemed to kill him twice. However, Ōnamuchi was revived by his mother, {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}}, and fled to the {{ill|Ne-no-kuni|en|Ne-no-kuni|WD=Q7555425|lt=Netherworld}}. After overcoming trials imposed by {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}}, Ōnamuchi married {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime|ja|須勢理毘売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseribime|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スセリビメ}} and returned to conquer the Yasogami, becoming the great deity Ōkuninushi. Although Yagami Hime was welcomed to his palace, she feared the jealousy of Suseribime, the principal wife, and fled back to Inaba, leaving her newborn son, {{ill|Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimata no Kami|qq=}}, in a tree fork.
The nearby {{ill|Dake Kofun|ja|嶽古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dake Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is considered Yagami Hime’s burial site. Numerous legends about her exist across Japan.
The name "Yagami" likely refers to present-day {{ill|Yazu District, Tottori|en|Yazu District, Tottori|WD=Q1043163|lt=Yazu District}}, and her lack of the suffix "Mikoto" suggests she might have been a priestess of Inaba.<ref>Shinchosha, ''Shincho Nihon Koten Shusei Kojiki''</ref>
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (according to the ''Kojiki''). Blue indicates male deities, red indicates female deities, and yellow indicates unspecified gender.]] Yagami Hime bore one child with Ōkuninushi, {{ill|Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimata no Kami|qq=}}.
== Shrines == * {{ill|Ushinuma Shrine|ja|売沼神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushinuma Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Tottori|ja|鳥取県鳥取市河原町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * {{ill|Tsunamitada Kamijinja|ja|都波只知上神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunamitada Kamijinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tottori Prefecture) * {{ill|Shimamiko Shrine|ja|島御子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimamiko Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Tsushima|ja|長崎県対馬市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * {{ill|Yagami Hime Shrine|ja|八上姫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagami Hime Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Izumo|ja|島根県出雲市斐川町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}})
== Notes == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hare of Inaba|en|Hare of Inaba|WD=Q2330988|lt=Hare of Inaba}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|en|List of Japanese deities|WD=Q3240847|lt=List of Japanese deities}} * {{ill|Dake Kofun|ja|嶽古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dake Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:八上比売]][[zh:八上比賣]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:八上比売]][[zh:八上比賣]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:八上比売]][[zh:八上比賣]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|spouse={{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}|children={{ill|Ki no mata no kami|WD=Q11092833}}}} {{infobox deity | name = Yagami Hime | predecessor = | successor = | type = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | full name = 八上比売 (Yagami Hime) | other names = 稲羽八上姫 (Inaba Yagami Hime) | alternate = | Rank = | role = Unknown | mausoleum = | father = Unknown | mother = | relatives = | consort ={{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=大国主}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}}| children = {{ill|Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimata no Kami|qq=}} | shrine = {{ill|Ushinuma Shrine|ja|売沼神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushinuma Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and others | gender= {{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}} | offspring= {{ill|Ki no mata no kami|WD=Q11092833}} }}
'''Yagami Hime''' (八上比売 / 八上姫, also read as Yagami Hime or Yakami Hime) is a goddess from {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology}}, associated with {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Yazu District|ja|八上郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yazu District|1=Yazu District|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10891904}}. She was the first consort of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=大国主}}.
== Overview ==
In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, her name is written as '''Yagami Hime''', while in the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}}, she is referred to as '''Inaba Yagami Hime'''.
Ōnamuchi no Mikoto (Ōkuninushi) traveled to {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a servant to his many brothers, the {{ill|Yasogami|ja|八十神|1=Yasogami|lt=Yasogami (80 gods)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}, who were seeking Yagami Hime’s hand in marriage. Along the way, Ōnamuchi encountered a wounded hare that had been tricked and harmed by the Yasogami. He healed the hare’s injuries, and in return, the hare prophesied that Ōnamuchi would succeed in his courtship—a prediction that proved accurate.<ref>Takeuchi, Tsuneyasu. ''Modern Language Kojiki: Pocket Edition.'' Gakken Plus, 2016. ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6</ref>
Yagami Hime rejected the Yasogami's proposals and chose to marry Ōnamuchi instead. Jealous, the Yasogami schemed to kill him twice. However, Ōnamuchi was revived by his mother, {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}}, and fled to the {{ill|Ne-no-kuni|en|Ne-no-kuni|WD=Q7555425|lt=Netherworld}}. After overcoming trials imposed by {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}}, Ōnamuchi married {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime|ja|須勢理毘売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseribime|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スセリビメ}} and returned to conquer the Yasogami, becoming the great deity Ōkuninushi. Although Yagami Hime was welcomed to his palace, she feared the jealousy of Suseribime, the principal wife, and fled back to Inaba, leaving her newborn son, {{ill|Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimata no Kami|qq=}}, in a tree fork.
The nearby {{ill|Dake Kofun|ja|嶽古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dake Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is considered Yagami Hime’s burial site. Numerous legends about her exist across Japan.
The name "Yagami" likely refers to present-day {{ill|Yazu District, Tottori|en|Yazu District, Tottori|WD=Q1043163|lt=Yazu District}}, and her lack of the suffix "Mikoto" suggests she might have been a priestess of Inaba.<ref>Shinchosha, ''Shincho Nihon Koten Shusei Kojiki''</ref>
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (according to the ''Kojiki''). Blue indicates male deities, red indicates female deities, and yellow indicates unspecified gender.]] Yagami Hime bore one child with Ōkuninushi, {{ill|Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimata no Kami|qq=}}.
== Shrines == * {{ill|Ushinuma Shrine|ja|売沼神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushinuma Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Tottori|ja|鳥取県鳥取市河原町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * {{ill|Tsunamitada Kamijinja|ja|都波只知上神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunamitada Kamijinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tottori Prefecture) * {{ill|Shimamiko Shrine|ja|島御子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimamiko Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Tsushima|ja|長崎県対馬市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * {{ill|Yagami Hime Shrine|ja|八上姫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagami Hime Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Izumo|ja|島根県出雲市斐川町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}})
== Notes == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hare of Inaba|en|Hare of Inaba|WD=Q2330988|lt=Hare of Inaba}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|en|List of Japanese deities|WD=Q3240847|lt=List of Japanese deities}} * {{ill|Dake Kofun|ja|嶽古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dake Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

'''Yagami Hime''' (八上比売 / 八上姫, also read as Yagami Hime or Yakami Hime) is a goddess from {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology|1=Japanese mythology}}, associated with {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Yazu District|ja|八上郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yazu District|1=Q10891904|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10891904}}. She was the first consort of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=大国主}}.
== Overview ==
In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, her name is written as '''Yagami Hime''', while in the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}, she is referred to as '''Inaba Yagami Hime'''.
Ōnamuchi no Mikoto (Ōkuninushi) traveled to {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a servant to his many brothers, the {{ill|Yasogami|ja|八十神|1=Yasogami|lt=Yasogami (80 gods)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}, who were seeking Yagami Hime’s hand in marriage. Along the way, Ōnamuchi encountered a wounded hare that had been tricked and harmed by the Yasogami. He healed the hare’s injuries, and in return, the hare prophesied that Ōnamuchi would succeed in his courtship—a prediction that proved accurate.<ref>Takeuchi, Tsuneyasu. ''Modern Language Kojiki: Pocket Edition.'' Gakken Plus, 2016. ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6</ref>
Yagami Hime rejected the Yasogami's proposals and chose to marry Ōnamuchi instead. Jealous, the Yasogami schemed to kill him twice. However, Ōnamuchi was revived by his mother, {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}}, and fled to the {{ill|Ne-no-kuni|en|Ne-no-kuni|WD=Q7555425|lt=Netherworld|1=Ne-no-kuni}}. After overcoming trials imposed by {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}}, Ōnamuchi married {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime|ja|須勢理毘売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseribime|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スセリビメ}} and returned to conquer the Yasogami, becoming the great deity Ōkuninushi. Although Yagami Hime was welcomed to his palace, she feared the jealousy of Suseribime, the principal wife, and fled back to Inaba, leaving her newborn son, {{ill|Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimata no Kami|qq=}}, in a tree fork.
The nearby {{ill|Dake Kofun|ja|嶽古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dake Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is considered Yagami Hime’s burial site. Numerous legends about her exist across Japan.
The name "Yagami" likely refers to present-day {{ill|Yazu District, Tottori|en|Yazu District, Tottori|WD=Q1043163|lt=Yazu District|1=Yazu District, Tottori}}, and her lack of the suffix "Mikoto" suggests she might have been a priestess of Inaba.<ref>Shinchosha, ''Shincho Nihon Koten Shusei Kojiki''</ref>
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (according to the ''Kojiki''). Blue indicates male deities, red indicates female deities, and yellow indicates unspecified gender.]] Yagami Hime bore one child with Ōkuninushi, {{ill|Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata-no-Kami|lt=Kimata no Kami|ja|木俣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimata no Kami|qq=}}.
== Shrines == * {{ill|Ushinuma Shrine|ja|売沼神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushinuma Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Tottori|ja|鳥取県鳥取市河原町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * {{ill|Tsunamitada Kamijinja|ja|都波只知上神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunamitada Kamijinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tottori Prefecture) * {{ill|Shimamiko Shrine|ja|島御子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimamiko Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Tsushima|ja|長崎県対馬市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * {{ill|Yagami Hime Shrine|ja|八上姫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagami Hime Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Izumo|ja|島根県出雲市斐川町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}})
== Notes == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hare of Inaba|en|Hare of Inaba|WD=Q2330988|lt=Hare of Inaba|1=Hare of Inaba}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|en|List of Japanese deities|WD=Q3240847|lt=List of Japanese deities|1=List of Japanese deities}} * {{ill|Dake Kofun|ja|嶽古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dake Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
